description_long: This house features a compact, fortress-like design with two prominent cylindrical towers flanking the main structure. The exterior walls are rendered in a reddish-brown material, resembling brick or stone, and the towers are topped with dark, conical roofs. Small, evenly spaced windows are visible on the towers, while the central section includes a large arched entryway. The overall form is symmetrical, with the towers providing a sense of strength and enclosure. The roof surfaces are steeply pitched, suggesting effective water runoff and a traditional architectural influence. The house appears solid and robust, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on functional, defensive features.
description: A compact, fortress-like house with two cylindrical towers, reddish-brown walls, and dark conical roofs. The design is symmetrical and robust, with small windows and a large arched entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entryway between two cylindrical towers with conical roofs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the symmetry of the towers and the solid, windowed walls.
description_view_2: Rear view with the two towers and central section, lacking a visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the symmetry and window placement of the other side.
attribute_entryway_design: Large central arched doorway with no porch, flanked by cylindrical towers.
